design: 5 hand-authored banks x 24 templates; every word receives all 24 templates of its bank (deterministic, no sampling); slot {w} substituted verbatim; charStart/charEnd = span of the word; random-pool candidates reuse the same bank templates. Fail-closed: templates contain no probe word (exact-form check).

afraid.text: Maria was very afraid that day.
The old man seemed afraid when we spoke to him.

You seem afraid today, my sister said quietly.

Being afraid is hard for anyone.
Nobody wanted to admit they were afraid.
The young woman appeared afraid at the meeting.
After the storm, the farmer was still afraid.
Our coach was clearly afraid this morning.
The baby seemed less afraid once the music played.
His brother had always been afraid.
Those two sisters were both afraid back then.
The king grew afraid as the years passed.
Why is your uncle so afraid lately?

begin.text: She didn't want to begin the old clock.
He said he would begin it before dinner.

Would you begin this for me tomorrow?

No one dared to begin the stone wall.
We might begin the whole thing next week.
To begin well, you need patience.

She hoped to begin it without any trouble.
Every child manages to begin in a different way.
They never meant to begin anything at all.
